title: Remove Dictionary Items
content:
### Removing Items

---

### `.pop()` -- Remove by Key

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "A"}
age = student.pop("age")
print(age)       # 25 (returns the value)
print(student)   # {"name": "Alice", "grade": "A"}
```

> **Error:** Raises `KeyError` if key does not exist.

Provide a **default** to avoid errors:

```python
student.pop("missing", "not found")  # Returns "not found"
```

---

### `.popitem()` -- Remove Last Inserted

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}
item = student.popitem()
print(item)      # ("age", 25) (returns tuple)
print(student)   # {"name": "Alice"}
```

---

### `del` Keyword

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "A"}
del student["grade"]  # Remove specific key
del student            # Delete entire dictionary!
```

---

### `.clear()` -- Empty the Dictionary

```python
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25}
student.clear()
print(student)  # {}
```

---

### Summary

| Method | Description | Returns | Error if Missing |
|--------|-------------|---------|------------------|
| `.pop(key)` | Remove by key | Value | Yes |
| `.pop(key, default)` | Remove by key | Value or default | No |
| `.popitem()` | Remove last item | (key, value) tuple | Yes (if empty) |
| `del dict[key]` | Remove by key | Nothing | Yes |
| `.clear()` | Remove all | Nothing | No |

> **Best Practice:** Use `.pop()` when you need the removed value. Use `del` when you just want to remove. Use `.pop()` with a default when the key might not exist.
codeExample:
student = {"name": "Alice", "age": 25, "grade": "A"}

# pop
age = student.pop("age")
print("Popped age:", age)
print("Remaining:", student)

# popitem
item = student.popitem()
print("Popped item:", item)

# clear
student.clear()
print("After clear:", student)

quiz.question: Which method removes a key and returns its value?
